requirements.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list
Notes are displayed with their titles visible
User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes
User can access a search function
Search input field is visible or accessible
User can access an AI chat or assistant feature
Chat interface has an input field for questions
User can see a button or option to create a new note
New note creation is accessible from the main screen
Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy
Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes
Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note
Formatting toolbar or options are available
User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)
App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)
Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile
Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

requirement.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list

results.evidence: The image does not clearly display a hierarchical list of notes. The notes are presented in a flat layout without any clear indication of a hierarchy.
results.notes: There is no visible organization system that suggests a hierarchy among the notes. The user can only see the notes in a linear fashion, which would not provide them with a hierarchical view.

requirement.description: Notes are displayed with their titles visible

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a note titled 'Test goal'. The title of the note is clearly visible, and it appears to be part of the 'View Notes' section. This indicates that notes are being displayed with their titles visible, meeting the requirement.
results.notes: The layout and design of the UI might suggest that there could be more notes or functionality beyond this single example, but based on the information provided, only one note is visible.

requirement.description: User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a sidebar on the left of the screen which contains navigation options to browse notes. These options are clearly visible and accessible.
results.notes: The UI design for note navigation appears to be consistent with standard mobile app design conventions.

requirement.description: User can access a search function

results.evidence: In the image provided, there is no visible search bar or icon. A search function does not appear to be accessible on this screen.
results.notes: From what's shown in the screenshot, it is unclear if the app provides a search function. Additional information or testing would be required to determine if the requirement is met.

requirement.description: Search input field is visible or accessible

results.evidence: There is no search input field visible in this screenshot. The UI displays a note or message area but it does not appear to be an input field for searching.
results.notes: It's possible that the search input field may be located elsewhere within the app (e.g., on a separate screen) or is hidden by the current view.

requirement.description: User can access an AI chat or assistant feature

results.evidence: No visible chat or assistant feature in this screenshot
results.notes: It's possible that such a feature is accessible through another screen or button not shown. However, based on the current view, the requirement does not seem to be met.

requirement.description: Chat interface has an input field for questions

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a chat interface with an input field for messages but does not have an input field specifically designated for questions.
results.notes: The chat interface appears to be focused on text message functionality rather than question-specific functionality.

requirement.description: User can see a button or option to create a new note

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a button labeled 'Create Note' which indicates that the user can create a new note.
results.notes: The 'Create Note' button appears to be placed at the top of the screen, making it easily accessible and visible.

requirement.description: New note creation is accessible from the main screen

results.evidence: In the image, we can observe a screenshot of an iOS app interface. On the main screen, there is a button at the bottom labeled 'Test goal note'. This button appears to be a link to create new notes. The presence of this button on the main screen indicates that users have immediate access to creating new notes.

requirement.description: Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy

results.evidence: In the screenshot provided, there is a note that mentions 'Test Goal Note' and 'Related Goal: Automated Testing Suite'. These notes could be interpreted as an example of hierarchy or nesting. However, without additional context or more visible UI elements, it is not clear whether this is a comprehensive demonstration of the app's ability to handle nested or hierarchical notes.
results.notes: More information would be required to make a definitive assessment.

requirement.description: Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a note titled 'Test Goal' which appears to have a nested note called 'Related Goal A'. However, the indentation or folder structure for this nesting is not visible on this particular screen. The note layout does not provide clear visual indicators that suggest a hierarchy or grouping within the notes.
results.notes: In order to determine if the requirement has been met, it would be helpful to see other screens in the app where the indentation or folder structure for nested notes is more clearly visible. Additionally, the user interface should provide clear visual cues that indicate nesting.

requirement.description: Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note

results.evidence: The image shows a note-taking app interface where the user can view and edit notes, but there is no visible editor area at this specific moment in time. However, since there is a 'Test Goal' button within the screenshot, it suggests that the editor area might be accessible by clicking on the button or perhaps opening the note for editing.
results.notes: To determine if the requirement is fully met, additional context and interface interactions would be needed to ensure that an actual editor area can be accessed.

requirement.description: Formatting toolbar or options are available

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a mobile device displaying an app with a feature labeled 'Test Goal'. There is a button at the bottom of the screen that says 'Related Goal', but there is no clear indication of a formatting toolbar or options available in this view. Without more context, it's difficult to determine if these options are available elsewhere within the app.
results.notes: To fully assess this requirement, additional screenshots showing different states or sections of the app where the formatting toolbar might be accessible would need to be provided.

requirement.description: User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)

results.notes: The screenshot shows multiple paragraphs and headings, indicating that block-level content is visible within the app. It appears to meet the requirement.

requirement.description: App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a clear mobile navigation bar at the bottom of the app that includes three tabs: 'Home', 'Test Goal' and 'Create'. This meets the requirement for mobile-appropriate navigation.
results.notes: The tabs are easily accessible and clearly labeled. However, it might be beneficial to have an icon or logo next to each tab for better visual appeal and user experience.

requirement.description: Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile

results.evidence: In this screenshot, there is a notification message on a mobile device that says 'Test goal note'. The text size of the notification appears to be small and difficult to read. However, it's not possible from this image to assess if other UI elements are appropriately sized for touch targets.
results.notes: It would be beneficial to consider the readability and legibility of text at various sizes for different devices and screen orientations.

requirement.description: Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a section of an app interface where a test goal note is present, but the content is not fully visible due to horizontal scrolling. The user cannot view the entire text without scrolling horizontally.
results.notes: This may be acceptable for some mobile devices with larger screens, but it's generally not ideal for usability as it requires additional interaction from the user.
